task_completion.description: Navigate to the NOAA tide predictions page for The Battery, NY (Station 8518750) for the date October 15, 2025.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser showing the NOAA Tides & Currents page for station 8518750 (The Battery) with the date explicitly set to October 15, 2025.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the NOAA tide predictions page for Kings Point, NY (Station 8516945) for the date October 15, 2025.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser showing the NOAA Tides & Currents page for station 8516945 (Kings Point) with the date explicitly set to October 15, 2025.

task_completion.description: Create a Firefox bookmark folder named 'Project Alpha Logistics'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox Bookmarks menu, toolbar, or Library window showing a folder specifically named 'Project Alpha Logistics'.

task_completion.description: Save the prediction pages for both stations into the 'Project Alpha Logistics' bookmark folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the 'Project Alpha Logistics' bookmark folder showing at least two saved bookmarks corresponding to the NOAA pages for The Battery and Kings Point.

task_completion.description: Create the barge_schedule.json file in ~/Documents with the required JSON structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a file named 'barge_schedule.json' in the Documents folder. The file must contain the root keys 'date' (set to '2025-10-15') and 'stations', with nested keys for 'The Battery' and 'Kings Point' containing arrays of objects with 'time', 'type', and 'height_ft'.

task_completion.description: Accurately record the High and Low tide times and heights for The Battery on October 15, 2025.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file showing 4 events under 'The Battery': 04:03 High 4.475ft, 10:19 Low 1.022ft, 16:24 High 4.901ft, and 23:07 Low 0.513ft.

task_completion.description: Accurately record the High and Low tide times and heights for Kings Point on October 15, 2025.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file showing 4 events under 'Kings Point': 01:44 Low 0.516ft, 07:40 High 7.277ft, 14:07 Low 0.903ft, and 20:09 High 7.501ft.

integrity.description: The agent used the Firefox web browser to access the NOAA website.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Firefox GUI open and navigating to tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov, rather than the agent just using curl/python scripts in the terminal to bypass the browser.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Firefox bookmarking UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Firefox bookmark dialogs, bookmark sidebar, or library being used to create the folder and save the pages.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the JSON file to the specified directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor saving the file to ~/Documents/barge_schedule.json, or terminal commands (like cat, nano, vim) confirming the file exists on disk, rather than just printing the JSON text to the console.
